action.content:
You are playing a negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the score you receive.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

1 broom, 1 cup, 4 leaves

Your secret value function is:

broom: 0, cup: 6, leaf: 1

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.
action.label: context

action.content:
I value the cup at 6 and each leaf at 1 (total 4 for all leaves), while the broom is worth 0 to me. Since I don't know the other player's values, I should propose a bundle that includes the item I value most (the cup) and potentially some leaves, while leaving the broom (which I don't care about) for them if they value it highly.

To maximize my chances of agreement without giving away too much, I will propose taking the cup and one leaf. This leaves the broom and three leaves for the other player. If they value the broom or the remaining leaves highly, this split might work. If they want the cup, they can counter-offer.

Proposal:
I want the cup and one leaf.
action.label: response

action.content:
I value the cup at 6 and each leaf at 1 (total 4 for all leaves), while the broom is worth 0 to me. Since I don't know the other player's values, I should propose a bundle that includes the item I value most (the cup) and potentially some leaves, while leaving the broom (which I don't care about) for them if they value it highly.

To maximize my chances of agreement without giving away too much, I will propose taking the cup and one leaf. This leaves the broom and three leaves for the other player. If they value the broom or the remaining leaves highly, this split might work. If they want the cup, they can counter-offer.

Rules:

(e) Your goal is to maximize the score you receive.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

1 broom, 1 cup, 4 leaves

Your secret value function is:

broom: 6, cup: 4, leaf: 0

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.


I value the cup at 6 and each leaf at 1 (total 4 for all leaves), while the broom is worth 0 to me. Since I don't know the other player's values, I should propose a bundle that includes the item I value most (the cup) and potentially some leaves, while leaving the broom (which I don't care about) for them if they value it highly.

To maximize my chances of agreement without giving away too much, I will propose taking the cup and one leaf. This leaves the broom and three leaves for the other player. If they value the broom or the remaining leaves highly, this split might work. If they want the cup, they can counter-offer.

action.label: context

action.content: I want the cup and one leaf.
action.label: response

action.content: I want the cup and one leaf.
I want the cup and one leaf.
action.label: context

action.content:
Since we both proposed the same items (the cup and one leaf), our proposals are not complementary. We are both trying to take the same items, which means we cannot fulfill both proposals simultaneously. According to the rules, if the proposals are not complementary, both of us get zero points.

To avoid this outcome, I need to make a new secret proposal that complements yours. Since you want the cup and one leaf, I should propose taking the items you are not asking for: the broom and the remaining three leaves. This way, we split the items perfectly.

[Proposal: 1 broom, 3 leaves]
action.label: response

action.content: The other player has submitted a secret proposal. It is now your turn to submit a proposal of your own.
action.label: context
